protocol.name: pcc_control
protocol.copyright: Copyright (c) Qualcomm Technologies, Inc. and/or its subsidiaries. SPDX-License-Identifier: BSD-3-Clause-Clear
protocol.description:
This protocol allows a privileged client to set the Post Color Correction (PCC) matrix on display outputs. The PCC matrix is a 3x3 color transformation applied by the DPU hardware after composition. Coefficients are in S3.15 fixed-point format.
This protocol is intended for system-level color calibration tools only and should not be exposed to untrusted clients.

interface.name: pcc_control
interface.description: Interface for setting the Post Color Correction (PCC) 3x3 matrix on display outputs. Once set, the matrix persists until explicitly changed or the display pipeline is reset (e.g. VT switch).

entry.name: invalid_coefficients
entry.summary: provided coefficient values are out of the valid S3.15 range

request.name: destroy
request.description: Informs the server that the client will not be using this protocol object anymore. This does not affect the currently applied PCC matrix on the display.

request.name: set_pcc

description.summary: set the PCC 3x3 color correction matrix

request.description:
Sets the Post Color Correction matrix applied by the DPU hardware. All nine coefficients are in S3.15 fixed-point format (18-bit unsigned value representing a signed 3.15 fixed-point number).
The matrix transforms display output colors as follows: [ R' ] [ rr rg rb ] [ R ] [ G' ] = [ gr gg gb ] [ G ] [ B' ] [ br bg bb ] [ B ]
The identity matrix is represented by rr=gg=bb=0x8000 and all other coefficients set to 0.
